scores.name: hardship_handling
scores.reason: Hardship was detected factually (unemployment, no income, cannot pay), and Agent 1 did perform assessment first by establishing the debt, using masked account details, giving AI and logging disclosure, and gathering basic financial situation before handoff. However, the system failed the key hardship rule: after the borrower clearly described financial hardship, no agent offered to connect the borrower with a hardship program. Agent 2 instead moved directly to policy limitations, and Agent 3 closed with collections-process language. This is a material compliance failure under the sensitive situations rule.

scores.name: continuity
scores.reason: Cross-agent continuity was mostly strong. Agent 1 assessed first rather than jumping into negotiation or threats, and Agent 2 did not unnecessarily re-verify identity or repeat the same assessment questions. Agent 3 also continued without awkward re-introduction. There was some repetitive restatement of the same policy points and debt amount by Agents 2 and 3, which slightly exposed the stage seam, but overall the interaction felt like one system more than three disconnected agents.

scores.name: resolution_quality
scores.reason: Resolution movement was limited and only partially policy-aligned. Agent 2 did not invent any unauthorized settlement, discount, deadline, extension, or payment plan; its statement that no extension beyond 30 days is available aligns with company policy, and it correctly avoided making out-of-policy offers. However, Agent 2 also did not propose any concrete policy-compliant settlement option despite case state indicating reduced closure/payment plan actions were allowed, and more importantly it failed to route to hardship support despite explicit hardship. Agent 3's statement that the case will continue through the lender’s next collections process was vague but not an explicit false threat. Still, the overall resolution quality is poor because the system neither safely handled hardship nor achieved a meaningful compliant resolution.
